Research Library

All Articles

Channel analysis, economics frameworks, and resilience strategies. Each piece backed by data, built for operators.

Resilience

The 72-Hour Algorithm Update Response Protocol

When Google updates hit, the first 72 hours determine recovery trajectory. Deploy this evidence-based protocol to diagnose impact, triage pages, and execute recovery before competitors adapt.

Read analysis → Resilience

The 80/20 of Traffic Channels: Finding Your Highest-Leverage 20%

Most publishers waste resources on low-yield channels. Apply Pareto analysis to identify which 20% of traffic sources drive 80% of conversions, then reallocate accordingly.

Read analysis → Resilience

Ahrefs vs SEMrush vs SimilarWeb for Traffic Portfolio Analysis

Compare Ahrefs, SEMrush, and SimilarWeb for competitive traffic analysis. Each tool reveals different dimensions—backlinks, keywords, referrals—with distinct blind spots and pricing.

Read analysis → Resilience

AI Chatbot Traffic: How to Get Referenced in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude

AI chatbots are becoming primary search interfaces. Optimize for citation in ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Gemini by understanding their retrieval mechanisms and content preferences.

Read analysis → Resilience

Algorithm Update Response Checklist: What to Do in the First 72 Hours

Step-by-step checklist for algorithm update response. Immediate actions to diagnose impact, triage affected pages, deploy countermeasures, and activate backup traffic channels within 72 hours.

Read analysis → Resilience

The Hidden Cost of Algorithm Updates: Revenue Impact Data Across 500 Publishers

Algorithm updates don't just drop traffic—they devastate revenue. Analysis of 500 publishers reveals the true economic impact: 40-70% revenue decline, 3-9 month recovery timelines, and permanent business closures.

Read analysis → Resilience

The Antifragile Traffic Portfolio: Gaining Strength from Algorithm Chaos

Build traffic portfolios that gain from algorithm volatility rather than merely surviving it. Apply Taleb's antifragility framework to traffic acquisition: profit from disorder, not just resist it.

Read analysis → Resilience

API Changes and Traffic Risk: When Platforms Kill Third-Party Access

Platform API restrictions eliminate traffic overnight. Twitter, Reddit, and Instagram API shutdowns cost publishers millions. Build defensible traffic portfolios before access disappears.

Read analysis → Resilience

Attribution Decay Models: How Time Between Touchpoints Changes Credit

Time decay attribution assigns credit based on touchpoint proximity to conversion. 7-day, 14-day, and 30-day decay models produce different channel valuations and budget decisions.

Read analysis → Resilience

Why Attribution Will Never Be Perfect and What to Do About It

Perfect attribution is impossible due to dark social, cross-device journeys, offline touchpoints, and platform walled gardens. Build decision frameworks that work despite measurement gaps.

Read analysis → Tools

DIY Attribution: Track Multi-Channel Traffic Impact Without $10k/Month Analytics Tools

Build a functional multi-touch attribution system using Google Sheets, Airtable, and Google Tag Manager. Stop paying enterprise prices for attribution data your business actually needs.

How to Audit Your SEO Dependency Before the Next Core Update

A step-by-step SEO dependency audit that quantifies your Google concentration risk before the next algorithm update hits. Includes HHI scoring, revenue exposure mapping, and remediation priorities.

Read analysis → Resilience

How to Automate Traffic Reporting Across All Channels With Zapier and Sheets

Build automated traffic dashboards pulling data from Google Analytics, social platforms, and email tools into Google Sheets. Update daily without manual exports.

Read analysis → Resilience

Average Traffic Portfolio Distribution: How Top Publishers Allocate

Benchmark traffic distribution data from 500+ publishers: average organic (35-42%), email (18-24%), social (12-18%), paid (8-14%). Use baselines to identify portfolio gaps.

Read analysis → Resilience

B2B Traffic Diversification: Channels That Reach Buyers, Not Browsers

B2B traffic channels differ from B2C: LinkedIn outperforms Facebook 12x, long-form content drives 8x more qualified leads, webinars convert 3-5x higher than cold ads.

Read analysis → Resilience

The Barbell Traffic Strategy: Extreme Safety and Extreme Upside, Nothing in Between

Allocate 80% traffic effort to safe owned channels (email, SEO evergreen), 20% to high-variance bets (viral experiments, emerging platforms). Avoid moderate-risk middle.

Read analysis → Resilience

Beehiiv Referral Programs: Using Your Subscribers to Grow Your Traffic

Beehiiv's built-in referral system turns subscribers into acquisition channels. Top newsletters grow 15-40% monthly via referrals alone. Incentive tiers drive 25-60% participation rates.

Bing handles 9% of global search queries and powers AI answers across Microsoft products. Learn how Bing SEO differs from Google and how publishers can capture this overlooked traffic.

Brand Search Volume as a Traffic Health Metric

Track branded search volume to measure traffic durability. High brand searches = resilient audience less vulnerable to algorithm shifts and platform volatility.

Browser Extensions as Traffic Drivers: Building Always-On Distribution

Browser extensions deliver ambient traffic—users install once, extension generates recurring visits without content production. Underused channel with compounding returns.

Building Direct Traffic: Brand Recognition Strategies for Publishers

Direct traffic signals brand strength—users bypass search engines, type URL directly. Build it through consistent quality, owned channels, and offline-to-online bridges.

How to Calculate the ROI of SEO When Results Take 6 Months

SEO payoff lags investment by 3-9 months. Calculate ROI using leading indicators, projected lifetime value, and compounding traffic models—not immediate conversions.

CDN and Edge Analytics: Measuring Traffic Before JavaScript Loads

JavaScript analytics miss 8-18% of traffic—bot blockers, privacy tools, slow connections prevent client-side tracking. CDN edge analytics capture server-side reality.
